2.note: Verses 5:2-20 are numbered 5:1-19 in Hebrew text.

9.anchor: Even the king milks the land for his own profit!
9.note: The meaning of the Hebrew in verses 8 and 9 is uncertain.

19.anchor: For people and animals share the same fate—both breathe
19.note: Or both have the same spirit.

1.anchor: These are the words of the Teacher,

12.anchor: So I decided to compare wisdom with foolishness and madness (for who can do this better than I, the king?
12.note: The meaning of the Hebrew is uncertain.

25.anchor: For who can eat or enjoy anything apart from him?
25.note: As in Greek and Syriac versions; Hebrew reads apart from me?

18.anchor: Pay attention to these instructions, for anyone who fears God will avoid both extremes.

26.anchor: I discovered that a seductive woman

10.anchor: I have seen wicked people buried with honor. Yet they were the very ones who frequented the Temple and are now praised
10.note: As in some Hebrew manuscripts and Greek version; many Hebrew manuscripts read and are forgotten.

10.anchor: The Teacher sought to find just the right words to express truths clearly.
10.note: Or sought to write what was upright and true.

11.anchor: The words of the wise are like cattle prods—painful but helpful. Their collected sayings are like a nail-studded stick with which a shepherd

2.anchor: The same destiny ultimately awaits everyone, whether righteous or wicked, good or bad,
2.note: As in Greek and Syriac versions and Latin Vulgate; Hebrew lacks or bad.

10.anchor: Whatever you do, do well. For when you go to the grave,

1.anchor: and in time, profits will flow back to you.
1.note: Or Give generously, / for your gifts will return to you later. Throw your bread on the waters, / for after many days you will find it again.

2.anchor: But divide your investments among many places,
2.note: Hebrew among seven or even eight.

5.anchor: Just as you cannot understand the path of the wind or the mystery of a tiny baby growing in its mother’s womb,
5.note: Some manuscripts read Just as you cannot understand how breath comes to a tiny baby in its mother’s womb.

16.anchor: What sorrow for the land ruled by a servant,

15.anchor: But then everyone rushes to the side of yet another youth

16.note: Hebrew There is no end to all the people, to all those who are before them.
